objective: Hide Profile, TJM, Suivi (Applications), and CV tabs behind a premium feature flag. These tabs are paid features that previously required authentication/payment.

content: Hide 4 tabs (profile, tjm, applications, cv) behind isPremium local flag
reason: User wants these paid features behind paywall

content: Use local chrome.storage to track isPremium status (no auth needed)
reason: No authentication exists anymore, premium status is local only

issues.description: Direct chrome.storage.local usage bypasses the facade/bridge pattern. All other state modules use shell facades (settings.facade.ts, app-flags.facade.ts) that route through the messaging bridge to the service worker. premium.svelte.ts calls chrome.storage.local directly, violating the established I/O pattern and the AGENTS.md rule: 'the side panel n'appelle JAMAIS directement IndexedDB ou chrome.cookies. Tout passe par le service worker.'

Premium pages are still rendered in the DOM via {#if nav.currentPage === 'profile'} etc. If nav.navigate('profile') were called programmatically (e.g. deep link, dev panel), the premium page would render without the tab being visible. Consider guarding those {#if} blocks with || premium.isPremium.

The implementation plan in context.md item 2 mentions creating premium-chrome-storage.ts separately, but the I/O was inlined into the state module. This is acceptable for a single boolean, but the naming discrepancy should be cleaned up in context.md.
No unit test for the premium state store (createPremiumStore). A pure-logic test verifying the factory returns getters and that isPremium defaults to false would be straightforward.

content: Fix FC&IS violation: premium.svelte.ts uses chrome.storage.local directly. Must use facade/bridge pattern like theme.svelte.ts. Also add try/catch to load(), and guard {#if} blocks in App.svelte with premium.isPremium check
